title: Conjecture 1.1
statement_text: Every triangle-free planar graph contains an induced 2-degenerate subgraph on at least $\frac{7}{8}$ of its vertices.
context_text: The authors introduce this as the central open problem motivating the paper, noting it would be tight for the cube (the unique 3-regular triangle-free planar graph on 8 vertices). The paper's main theorems (Theorems 1.2 and 1.3) prove weaker bounds of $\frac{4}{5}$ and $\frac{7}{8}n - 18n_3$ respectively as partial progress.
paper_title: Induced 2-degenerate Subgraphs of Triangle-free Planar Graphs

title: Informal belief: 5/6 bound attainable
statement_text: We believe the argument we use can be strengthened to give a bound $\frac{5}{6}$, however the technical issues are substantial and since we do not see this as a viable way to prove Conjecture 1.1 in full, we prefer to present the easier argument giving the bound $\frac{4}{5}$.
context_text: Immediately after stating Theorem 1.2 (the $\frac{4}{5}$ bound), the authors remark that their method is likely improvable to $\frac{5}{6}$ but that pursuing this is not a practical route to proving the full $\frac{7}{8}$ conjecture.
notes: Stated as an authorial belief in running prose, not a labelled environment.
paper_title: Induced 2-degenerate Subgraphs of Triangle-free Planar Graphs
